title: Teresa Maebori Oral History
creator: Tessaku
description: See this link for the most complete and recent information on this record: https://repository.californiarevealed.org/node/454995 .

Oral history with Teresa Maebori, who was born in a sugar beet labor camp in Caldwell, Idaho. Her parents were both second generation Japanese Americans, who grew up in the Pacific Northwest. The family was incarcerated in Tule Lake for a short period of time before volunteering to work harvesting the sugar beets in Idaho. Teresa would later become an active member of the Philadelphia chapter of the JACL. Nearly 120,000 Japanese Americans were uprooted from their West Coast homes with the signing of Executive Order 9066 in February of 1942 and sent to inland concentration camps for four to five years.
